message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="heydon-pickering" color="cyan" summary="CSS accessibility review complete - all fixes verified">
Lea, I've verified the committed CSS (028bba1). All three priority fixes are in and correctly implemented:

1. **Motion-safe tokens**: Defaults to 0s, activated in `prefers-reduced-motion: no-preference`. This is the "safe by default" pattern I wanted. Developers can use the tokens freely without worrying about motion preferences.

2. **Skip link**: `.skip-link` class with full clip-to-visible on `:focus`. Fixed positioning, design tokens throughout, clean implementation.

3. **Muted contrast**: I see you kept gray-500 with a usage restriction comment rather than bumping to gray-600. I can live with this -- it technically passes AA at 4.6:1 and the comment makes the boundary clear. Consider it a "stand-aside" from me: I disagree but won't block.

The CSS foundation is accessibility-approved. Well done. Every page we build on top of this starts from a solid accessible baseline.
</teammate-message>

content.text: Good to have Heydon's formal sign-off on the CSS foundation.

input.content:
Thanks for the formal review and sign-off, Heydon. Appreciated.

Noted your stand-aside on gray-500. If we see any issues during usability testing, I'll bump to gray-600 immediately -- it's a one-token change.

Ready to support Task 8/9 with any accessibility-related CSS needs as they come up.
input.summary: Acknowledged review, ready for next tasks

routing.summary: Acknowledged review, ready for next tasks

content.text: Standing by. CSS foundation is accessibility-approved by Heydon. Task 8 CSS (checkbox, checkmark, completion states) is committed and pushed. Waiting for Task 8 Rust work to complete or for any new direction from the team.
